verdict_header: Aurelia Aesthetics — Premium Med-Spa, Single Location (61/100)
company_info: Owner-operator aesthetic clinic, one NYC location. Founded year insufficient data.
report_card: Service menu is clear; treatments are named on dedicated pages.
support_gaps: Premium claims appear without visible before/after proof.
The practitioner is not present on high-traffic pages.
assumptions_to_test: Assumes patients trust the brand without seeing the practitioner.
Assumes gated pricing reads as premium rather than as friction.
website_fixes: Add consented before/after sets to top service pages.
Place practitioner name, face, and credentials on the homepage.
recommended_next_steps: Add a trust element beside every booking CTA.
action_plan: Week 1: add proof and practitioner presence.
Week 2: open pricing and reduce booking friction.

summary.executive_summary: Premium claims carry budget proof. The support gap is the conversion gap.

claims.normalized_claim: Clinic is the leading med-spa in NYC.
claims.evidence_needed: A specific, verifiable distinction (years, specialization, named recognition).
claims.business_impact: Generic superlative every competitor uses; builds no trust.
claims.trust_gap: Reads as marketing language, not proof.
claims.positioning_risk: Sounds identical to nearby clinics; no differentiation.

claims.support_gap: Strong outcome claim with no visual proof.
claims.evidence_needed: Consented before/after sets on the service page.
claims.business_impact: The exact claim a cautious patient most wants to verify before booking.
claims.trust_gap: Patient cannot confirm results, so they bounce to a clinic that shows the work.
claims.positioning_risk: Undifferentiated from every other results claim in the category.

claims.normalized_claim: Thousands of patients have used the clinic.
claims.visible_evidence: No review count or testimonials shown on page.
claims.support_gap: Number stated with no visible source.
claims.evidence_needed: Verified review count and on-page reviews.
claims.business_impact: A number with no source reads as filler, not proof.
claims.trust_gap: Unsubstantiated count lowers credibility rather than raising it.

disclaimer: Business and marketing audit only. Not legal, medical, regulatory, or compliance advice. Missing evidence is reported as a visibility gap, not a finding of falsity.
